name: Bing Crosby
abstract: Harry Lillis "Bing" Crosby Jr. (May 3, 1903 – October 14, 1977) was an American singer and actor.

about_quotes.speaker: Laurence Juber
about_quotes.evidence: his dad, Perry Botkin Sr. was Bing Crosby’s musical director for 17 years... Perry was kind of the link between the New York jazz studio guitar players, like Eddie Lang, and the L.A. studio players.
about_quotes.doc_title: Laurence Juber interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, July 21, 2020 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy and Jeannie Cheatham
about_quotes.evidence: INTERVIEWER mentions watching 'High Society' with Bing Crosby and Louis Armstrong; Interviewee does not mention any personal interaction with Bing Crosby.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy and Jeannie Cheatham interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 12, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Louis Armstrong
about_quotes.evidence: I guess in some of those old radio shows you did with Crosby... Well, with cats like Bing and Frank, how can you feel other way but just to get together?

about_quotes.speaker: Bobby Gordon
about_quotes.evidence: we got interested in Bing Crosby’s singing because he had a very nice voice... we enjoyed Bing’s style of jazz... It’s kind of like a tribute to him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bobby Gordon interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 12, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Conrad "Connie" Jones
about_quotes.evidence: It was a song called Bessie Couldn't Help It that was done, if I'm not mistaken, by Bing Crosby with the Paul Whiteman band.

about_quotes.speaker: Maxine Sullivan
about_quotes.evidence: The first rendition of it I heard, or maybe at the same time, was Bing Crosby made a recording of "Down The Old Ox Road."
